title: Parenting Skills Classes * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parenting Skills Classes * Native American Community 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community At Risk Families Child Abuse Issues Disabilities Issues Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Domestic Violence Issues Fathers Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Sexual Assault Issues Substance Use Disorder Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment and evaluation, counseling (individual, couples, family, and group), case management, cultural groups and activities, Native parenting classes, foster parent groups, access to physical health services, Healthy Native Kids (OHP/CHIPRA) insurance assistance, and wraparound care for Native youth with multiple needs. Referrals to other resources provided. Also provides diapers. Staff receive training on cultural competence to provide cultural programming for clients. Staff includes Master's-level counselors and social workers, certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADC), psychologists and child psychiatrists, and nurse practitioners. Student interns studying to become CADCs or counselors may also work under supervision of licensed staff. The program is certified by the State of Oregon to provide mental health and addiction treatment services for all ages. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Wraparound care: Youth who identify as Native American ages 9-21 All other services: Families with minor children who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps Native youth and families involved with foster care by offering cultural support, connecting them to community resources, and assisting with reunification or finding permanent homes. Services include group meetings, support classes, and family events. Also recruits and supports Native American foster families, guiding them through the certification process. Services for foster parents include Positive Indian Parenting classes, Sibling and Family Night events, and help with genealogy and regalia. Does not provide legal advice. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American youth ages 0-24 who are in state or tribal foster care systems, their birth parents, and their foster parents. Young adults who have aged out of foster care can receive services through age 24. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER POSITIVE INDIAN PARENTING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Eight-week class providing culturally specific training for Native American parents and non-Native American foster parents of Native American children. Classes explore the values and attitudes expressed in traditional Native American child-treating practices and apply those values to modern skills in parenting. Provides childcare and and meal to program participants. Positive Indian Parenting was developed by the National Indian Child Welfare Association (NICWA) and is recognized by DHS for foster parent certification training requirements. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American community and families fostering Native American children Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT
